response:
# PRODUCT REQUIREMENT DOCUMENT - SPECIFICATION SECTIONS

## CURRENT STATE SUMMARY:
Currently, customers can access only the last 24 months of monthly statements within the app. For annual or consolidated statements, customers must contact customer support teams who manually retrieve archived statements and email them to the registered email ID. This creates operational overhead, delays (manual retrieval and sharing), and no self-service capability for comprehensive annual financial summaries needed for tax filing purposes.

---

## SCOPE:

### IN SCOPE:
- Annual statement generation for up to 3 financial years (FY)
- Asynchronous PDF generation with 30-minute SLA
- In-app view and download of annual statements
- Request mechanism through customer support agents
- Annual statement content: transaction summary, rewards summary, EMI details, add-on card information, credit limit history
- Password-protected PDF for email delivery
- Non-password-protected PDF for in-app access
- Financial year calculation based on Indian FY (April 1 - March 31)
- Request status tracking and notifications
- Retry mechanism for failed generation attempts

### OUT OF SCOPE:
- Calendar year statements — Indian tax filing requires financial year (April-March) format only
- Statements older than 3 financial years — regulatory retention requirements and server load constraints
- Real-time/synchronous statement generation — server load management requires asynchronous processing
- Customizable date range statements — complexity and server load; monthly statements already serve this need
- Physical/printed annual statement delivery — digital-only product as per existing SOP assumptions
- Annual statements for closed/cancelled accounts — focus on active customer tax filing needs for MVP
- Multi-year consolidated single PDF — each FY generates separate PDF to manage file size and processing load

---

## FUNCTIONAL USE CASES:

### Use Case 1: Self-service annual statement download (Happy Path)
**Actor:** Self-employed credit cardholder (primary user)

**Precondition:** 
- User has active HDFC PIXEL credit card
- User has completed at least one full financial year with the card
- User is authenticated in the PIXEL app

**User Story:** As a self-employed credit card user, I want to request and download my annual financial year statement from the app so that I can share it with my CA for tax filing within 30 minutes without contacting support.

**Steps:**
1. User navigates to Statements section in PIXEL app
2. User selects "Annual Statements" tab
3. System displays available financial years (up to 3 FYs)
4. User selects desired financial year (e.g., FY 2023-24)
5. User taps "Request Statement" button
6. System validates request and initiates asynchronous PDF generation
7. System displays "Request submitted - statement will be ready in 30 minutes" confirmation with request ID
8. System sends push notification when PDF generation completes
9. User returns to Annual Statements section
10. User taps "Download" button for the generated statement
11. System downloads password-protected PDF to device
12. User accesses PDF using card last 4 digits as password

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given user has completed FY 2023-24, when user views Annual Statements tab, then system displays "FY 2023-24" as available option with status "Not Requested"
- Given user requests FY 2023-24 statement at 10:00 AM, when generation completes, then user receives push notification by 10:30 AM with message "Your FY 2023-24 annual statement is ready to download"
- Given annual statement PDF is generated, when user downloads from app, then PDF contains: cover page with FY dates, credit limit summary, transaction summary by month, rewards earned and redeemed summary, EMI summary with outstanding amounts, add-on card transaction breakdown, and total spend by category
- Given user downloads annual statement PDF via email, when user attempts to open, then PDF requires password (last 4 digits of card number) to access
- Given user downloads annual statement from in-app, when user opens PDF, then no password is required as user is already authenticated
- Given user has requested statement for FY 2023-24, when user attempts to request same FY again within 24 hours, then system displays "Statement already requested - download available below" and shows existing PDF

**Failure State AC:**
- Given PDF generation fails due to server timeout, when 30 minutes elapse, then user receives notification "Statement generation delayed - we'll notify you when ready" and system auto-retries generation every 15 minutes for up to 3 attempts
- Given user has no transactions in selected FY, when user requests statement, then system displays error "No transactions found for FY 2022-23. Annual statement cannot be generated."

**Priority:** P0

---

### Use Case 2: Annual statement request via customer support (Edge Case)
**Actor:** Customer support agent (secondary user)

**Precondition:**
- Customer has called/chatted with support requesting annual statement
- Agent has verified customer identity through standard authentication
- Customer's registered email ID is active (not hard bounced)

**User Story:** As a customer support agent, I want to request and email annual statements on behalf of customers so that users who cannot access the app can still receive their tax filing documents within 30 minutes.

**Steps:**
1. Agent logs into Support Center/Ops Center
2. Agent searches customer by card number or mobile number
3. Agent navigates to "Statements" section in customer profile
4. Agent selects "Annual Statements" option
5. System displays available financial years and previous request history
6. Agent selects desired financial year(s) - up to 3 FYs
7. Agent confirms customer's registered email ID
8. Agent clicks "Request & Email Statement" button
9. System validates email ID (checks against hard bounce list)
10. System initiates asynchronous PDF generation with email delivery flag
11. System displays confirmation with request ID and estimated completion time
12. System generates password-protected PDF within 30 minutes
13. System sends email to customer's registered email ID with PDF attachment
14. System logs request in customer interaction history
15. Agent informs customer to check email in 30 minutes

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given agent requests FY 2023-24 statement for customer, when generation completes, then system sends email to registered email ID with subject "Your HDFC PIXEL Annual Statement for FY 2023-24" and password-protected PDF attachment
- Given customer's email ID has hard bounced previously, when agent attempts to request statement, then system displays error "Email delivery failed previously - please update customer email ID before requesting statement" and blocks request
- Given agent requests multiple FY statements (FY 2022-23, FY 2023-24), when generation completes, then system sends separate emails for each FY with respective PDF attachments
- Given statement generation completes, when email is sent, then email body contains: greeting, FY period, password instruction (last 4 digits of card), statement summary (total transactions, total spend, rewards earned), and support contact information
- Given agent requests statement, when request is logged, then system captures: agent ID, customer card number, FY requested, request timestamp, delivery method (email), and request status in interaction history

**Failure State AC:**
- Given email delivery fails with soft bounce, when system detects bounce, then system auto-retries email delivery after 2 hours for up to 3 attempts and updates request status to "Delivery Pending"

**Priority:** P0

---

### Use Case 3: Annual statement generation failure and recovery (Failure Case)
**Actor:** System (automated process)

**Precondition:**
- User or agent has requested annual statement generation
- Request has been queued in asynchronous processing system
- Server resources are under heavy load or data retrieval encounters errors

**User Story:** As the PIXEL platform system, I want to handle annual statement generation failures gracefully with automatic retries and clear user communication so that users receive their statements reliably even during high-load periods.

**Steps:**
1. System picks up annual statement generation request from queue
2. System begins fetching transaction data for requested FY from database
3. Database query times out after 5 minutes due to heavy load
4. System logs error with request ID, error code, and timestamp
5. System updates request status to "Generation Failed - Retry Scheduled"
6. System schedules automatic retry after 15 minutes
7. System sends notification to user: "Statement generation is taking longer than expected - we'll notify you when ready"
8. System attempts retry #1 after 15 minutes
9. If retry #1 fails, system schedules retry #2 after 30 minutes
10. If retry #2 fails, system schedules retry #3 after 60 minutes
11. If all 3 retries fail, system updates status to "Generation Failed - Contact Support"
12. System sends notification to user with support contact information
13. System creates automatic support ticket with error details
14. Support team receives alert for manual intervention

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given statement generation fails on first attempt, when system schedules retry, then request status updates to "Retry Scheduled" and user sees "Processing - estimated time 45 minutes" in app
- Given all 3 automatic retries fail, when final failure occurs, then system sends push notification "We couldn't generate your FY 2023-24 statement. Our team is working on it - you'll receive it via email within 2 hours" and creates support ticket with priority "High"
- Given generation fails due to missing transaction data, when system detects data integrity issue, then system logs error code "DATA_INCOMPLETE" and skips retry attempts, immediately creating support ticket for manual review
- Given 100 concurrent annual statement requests are queued, when system processes queue, then system throttles processing to maximum 20 concurrent generations to prevent server overload
- Given statement generation exceeds 30-minute SLA, when 35 minutes elapse, then system sends proactive notification "Your statement is still being prepared - we'll notify you shortly" and escalates to monitoring team

**Failure State AC:**
- Given system encounters critical database failure during generation, when error is detected, then system halts all annual statement processing, sends alert to engineering team, displays maintenance message "Annual statements temporarily unavailable - please try after 2 hours" to all users, and logs incident with severity "Critical"
- Given user's account has data corruption (null values in critical fields), when system attempts generation, then system fails gracefully with error "Unable to generate statement due to data issue - support team notified" and creates ticket with account details for data cleanup

**Priority:** P0

---

## NON-FUNCTIONAL USE CASES:

### Performance Requirements:
- **Annual Statement Generation SLA**: 95% of annual statement PDF generation requests must complete within 30 minutes from request initiation during normal load conditions (up to 1000 concurrent requests)
  - **Acceptance**: Load testing with 1000 concurrent requests shows 95th percentile completion time ≤30 minutes; monitoring dashboard tracks generation time distribution

- **PDF File Size Optimization**: Annual statement PDF file size must not exceed 5MB for FY with up to 10,000 transactions to ensure smooth download on mobile networks
  - **Acceptance**: Test generation with maximum transaction volume (10,000 transactions) produces PDF ≤5MB; compression algorithms applied maintain readability

- **Asynchronous Queue Processing**: System must handle up to 5000 annual statement requests per hour during peak tax season (March-April) without degradation
  - **Acceptance**: Load test with 5000 requests/hour shows queue processing maintains <30 min SLA; no request timeouts or dropped requests

- **App Response Time**: Annual statements list view must load within 2 seconds on 4G network connection
  - **Acceptance**: Performance testing on 4G network simulation shows p95 load time ≤2 seconds

### Security Requirements:
- **PDF Password Protection**: All annual statement PDFs delivered via email must be password-protected using last 4 digits of card number as password
  - **Acceptance**: Security audit confirms all email-delivered PDFs require password; automated test verifies PDF cannot be opened without correct password

- **In-App PDF Access Control**: Annual statement PDFs accessed within app must only be available to authenticated users with valid session tokens
  - **Acceptance**: Security testing confirms expired session tokens cannot access PDFs; unauthorized API calls return 401 error

- **Data Encryption in Transit**: Annual statement PDF downloads must use TLS 1.3 encryption for data in transit
  - **Acceptance**: Network traffic analysis confirms TLS 1.3 encryption; SSL Labs test shows A+ rating

- **PII Data Masking**: Annual statement PDFs must mask full card number (show only last 4 digits) and full account numbers per PCI-DSS compliance
  - **Acceptance**: Manual review of generated PDFs confirms card number displayed as "XXXX XXXX XXXX 1234"; compliance audit passes PCI-DSS requirements

### Accessibility Requirements:
- **PDF Screen Reader Compatibility**: Annual statement PDFs must be tagged for screen reader accessibility (PDF/UA standard)
  - **Acceptance**: PDF accessibility checker (PAC 3) validates PDF/UA compliance; screen reader testing (NVDA, TalkBack) successfully reads all content

- **Color Contrast Compliance**: All text in annual statement PDF must meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio standards (4.5:1 for normal text)
  - **Acceptance**: Color contrast analyzer confirms all text meets 4.5:1 ratio; accessibility audit passes WCAG 2.1 AA

### Reliability Requirements:
- **Retry Mechanism Success Rate**: Automatic retry mechanism must successfully generate statements in 90% of initially failed requests within 3 retry attempts
  - **Acceptance**: Monitor retry success rate over 30 days shows ≥90% success rate; failure analysis identifies root causes for remaining 10%

- **Email Delivery Success Rate**: Annual statement emails must achieve 95% delivery success rate (excluding hard bounces)
  - **Acceptance**: Email delivery monitoring shows ≥95% delivery rate; soft bounce retry mechanism recovers 80% of soft bounces within 24 hours

- **Data Consistency**: Annual statement transaction totals must match monthly statement totals within the same FY with 100% accuracy
  - **Acceptance**: Automated reconciliation script compares annual vs monthly statement totals for 1000 sample accounts; zero discrepancies found

---

## METRICS IMPACT:

### Metric 1: Support Ticket Volume - Statement Requests
- **Current baseline:** Unknown — check with analytics (estimated 500-800 tickets/month based on SOP indicating duplicate statement requests are common)
- **Expected impact:** 60-70% reduction in statement-related support tickets within 3 months post-launch as self-service adoption increases
- **Owner:** Customer Support Operations Team

### Metric 2: Tax Season User Engagement (March-April)
- **Current baseline:** Unknown — check with analytics (measure current app DAU/MAU during March-April period)
- **Expected impact:** 25-30% increase in app DAU during tax season (March-April) as self-employed users access annual statements for CA sharing
- **Owner:** Product Analytics Team

### Metric 3: Statement Download Success Rate
- **Current baseline:** Email statement delivery success rate: 92% (from SOP - accounting for bounces)
- **Expected impact:** Improve overall statement access success rate to 97% by providing dual channels (in-app + email) for annual statement delivery
- **Owner:** Platform Engineering Team

---

## NEW METRICS TO TRACK:

### Annual Statement Request Volume
- **Definition:** Total number of annual statement generation requests initiated per day/week/month, segmented by source (in-app self-service vs agent-assisted)
- **Target:** 5000+ requests during peak tax season month (March); 500-1000 requests during off-season months
- **Owner:** Product Analytics Team

### Annual Statement Generation SLA Adherence
- **Definition:** Percentage of annual statement PDF generation requests completed within 30-minute SLA, measured from request timestamp to PDF ready timestamp
- **Target:** ≥95% of requests complete within 30 minutes during normal load; ≥90% during peak load (March-April)
- **Owner:** Platform Engineering Team

### Annual Statement Download Completion Rate
- **Definition:** Percentage of generated annual statements that are successfully downloaded by users within 7 days of generation, calculated as (statements downloaded / statements generated) * 100
- **Target:** ≥80% download rate within 7 days of generation
- **Owner:** Product Analytics Team

### Annual Statement Generation Failure Rate
- **Definition:** Percentage of annual statement requests that fail after all retry attempts, requiring manual intervention, calculated as (failed requests after retries / total requests) * 100
- **Target:** <2% failure rate requiring manual intervention
- **Owner:** Platform Engineering Team

### Annual Statement Email Delivery Success Rate
- **Definition:** Percentage of annual statement emails successfully delivered to customer registered email IDs (excluding hard bounces), measured via email service provider delivery confirmations
- **Target:** ≥95% delivery success rate (excluding hard bounces); ≥85% including soft bounce recovery
- **Owner:** Platform Engineering Team

---

## REPORTS & EXTRACTS IMPACT:

### New Reports Needed:
- **Annual Statement Generation Summary Report
